items.question: Which social media platform is associated with the highest average anxiety scores among teens?
items.expected_answer: According to the dataset, TikTok-exclusive users demonstrate the highest average anxiety levels at 5.75/10. This exceeds the anxiety levels reported for Instagram-only users (5.67/10) and those using multiple platforms (5.49/10).
items.generated_answer: "Medium" interaction serves as a middle ground (Anxiety: 5.68, Stress: 5.48).

items.question: What is the specific statistical correlation between sleep hours and the presence of a depression label?
items.expected_answer: There is a negative correlation coefficient of -0.19 between sleep hours and the presence of a depression label. This represents the strongest correlation among all behavioral variables in the dataset.
items.generated_answer: A negative correlation coefficient of -0.19 exists between sleep hours and the presence of a depression label.

items.question: For teens with the highest tier of pre-sleep screen time, what is the reported average addiction level?
items.expected_answer: Teens in the highest bracket of pre-sleep screen time (2.5 to 3.0 hours) report an average addiction level of 5.78. This group also shows a higher incidence of depression markers compared to lower-usage groups.
items.generated_answer: 5.78, compared to 5.40 for those with less than 1 hour. - The incidence of the depression label is approximately 13% higher in the 2-3 hour screen time group than in the 1-2 hour group. - Academic performance shows a slight downward trend as pre-sleep screen time increases. - Over 60% of teens with high addiction levels (8-10) report at least 2.0 hours of screen time before bed. - For every one-hour decrease in sleep, there is a measurable upward trend in addiction levels and anxiety scores. - Individuals reporting the maximum sleep duration (9.0 hours) show a mean anxiety level approximately 15% lower than those reporting the minimum.

items.question: What is the average daily physical activity duration for adolescents identified in the "High-Risk" profile?
items.expected_answer: Adolescents categorized as "High-Risk" report an average of 0.95 hours of physical activity per day. This is lower than the overall dataset mean of 1.01 hours.
items.generated_answer: 1.01 hours vs 0.95 hours. - The GPA of the low-risk group is significantly higher (3.2 vs 2.6).

items.question: Compare the average anxiety and stress levels of teens with "low" social interaction levels to the dataset means.
items.expected_answer: Teens with "low" social interaction levels report average anxiety of 5.52 and stress of 5.35. These are the lowest averages among the three interaction categories (High, Medium, Low).
